generatedSentence: On the highland, a hefty task awaited a group of juveniles, some dork-like, others aspiring heroes. In the old cafeteria, adorned with stained glass, they were careful to escort a new diplomat, sporting curious earrings, through the building, avoiding the usual rallies. Their project, an odyssey into an adjoined library, was to decipher a sketchy artifact that promised achievements beyond their imagination. The homefront felt safe, but a ghastly presence loomed. Unbeknownst to them, a rogue satellite, once part of a milky defense, was about to hijack their peaceful vacation. An early earthquake and a clucky feeling forewarned of an external entity. A parallel universe's nymphette, known for her nymphomaniac tendencies, and a demon-like alchemist, prone to disagree, were behind the audacious plot, seeking to eliminate a plucky dwarf who held the testaments of their true identity. They were oblivious to the injury this would cause. The rocket engines roared, their thrusters creating flames that painted the nebula. The machine was designed to bushwhack through mapped stellar landscapes. A drunk dragons' breath, a testament to otherworldly whisky, caused injuries that were no allergy whatsoever. This was no rugby match; a tornado of torment descended, creating a falloff in power. Their black bear mascot, along with a sad ostrich, watched from within the incubator, unable to escape their custody. Yet, a small group of twenty-something followers, including a vassal and a drummer, refused to enjoy this sucky entertainment. With fluent resolve, they combined their efforts to eject a virus, a swift injection that rendered the enemy offside. The trawler, now a rocket-propelled vehicle, was a landmark in the Eastern climate. Wearing a cloak, the unlikely kicker secured the chronometer, ensuring no more fantastical doodle or misogyny would darken their nightlife, securing their freedom from torment.
panelSentences: On the highland, a hefty task awaited a group of juveniles, some dork-like, others aspiring heroes. In the old cafeteria, adorned with stained glass, they were careful to escort a new diplomat, sporting curious earrings, through the building, avoiding the usual rallies. Their project, an odyssey into an adjoined library, was to decipher a sketchy artifact that promised achievements beyond their imagination. The homefront felt safe, but a ghastly presence loomed.

Unbeknownst to them, a rogue satellite, once part of a milky defense, was about to hijack their peaceful vacation. An early earthquake and a clucky feeling forewarned of an external entity. A parallel universe's nymphette, known for her nymphomaniac tendencies, and a demon-like alchemist, prone to disagree, were behind the audacious plot, seeking to eliminate a plucky dwarf who held the testaments of their true identity. They were oblivious to the injury this would cause.

The rocket engines roared, their thrusters creating flames that painted the nebula. The machine was designed to bushwhack through mapped stellar landscapes. A drunk dragons' breath, a testament to otherworldly whisky, caused injuries that were no allergy whatsoever. This was no rugby match; a tornado of torment descended, creating a falloff in power. Their black bear mascot, along with a sad ostrich, watched from within the incubator, unable to escape their custody.

Yet, a small group of twenty-something followers, including a vassal and a drummer, refused to enjoy this sucky entertainment. With fluent resolve, they combined their efforts to eject a virus, a swift injection that rendered the enemy offside. The trawler, now a rocket-propelled vehicle, was a landmark in the Eastern climate. Wearing a cloak, the unlikely kicker secured the chronometer, ensuring no more fantastical doodle or misogyny would darken their nightlife, securing their freedom from torment.
rawPanelSentences: On the highland, a hefty task awaited a group of juveniles, some dork-like, others aspiring heroes. In the old cafeteria, adorned with stained glass, they were careful to escort a new diplomat, sporting curious earrings, through the building, avoiding the usual rallies. Their project, an odyssey into an adjoined library, was to decipher a sketchy artifact that promised achievements beyond their imagination. The homefront felt safe, but a ghastly presence loomed.

Unbeknownst to them, a rogue satellite, once part of a milky defense, was about to hijack their peaceful vacation. An early earthquake and a clucky feeling forewarned of an external entity. A parallel universe's nymphette, known for her nymphomaniac tendencies, and a demon-like alchemist, prone to disagree, were behind the audacious plot, seeking to eliminate a plucky dwarf who held the testaments of their true identity. They were oblivious to the injury this would cause.

The rocket engines roared, their thrusters creating flames that painted the nebula. The machine was designed to bushwhack through mapped stellar landscapes. A drunk dragons' breath, a testament to otherworldly whisky, caused injuries that were no allergy whatsoever. This was no rugby match; a tornado of torment descended, creating a falloff in power. Their black bear mascot, along with a sad ostrich, watched from within the incubator, unable to escape their custody.

Yet, a small group of twenty-something followers, including a vassal and a drummer, refused to enjoy this sucky entertainment. With fluent resolve, they combined their efforts to eject a virus, a swift injection that rendered the enemy offside. The trawler, now a rocket-propelled vehicle, was a landmark in the Eastern climate. Wearing a cloak, the unlikely kicker secured the chronometer, ensuring no more fantastical doodle or misogyny would darken their nightlife, securing their freedom from torment.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A protagonist wearing a lime-green cape and a silver chrome helmet. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist, a young diplomat with glowing earrings, carefully escorts a group of juveniles through a futuristic cafeteria with stained-glass windows, towards an ancient, glowing artifact in an adjoined library. Wide shot, establishing scene. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist looks up, startled, as a dark, angular satellite streaks across a starry, milky sky, beginning to hijack an idyllic floating island vacation resort. Medium shot, dynamic angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist in their rocket, its engines blazing with neon flames through a turbulent nebula, narrowly avoiding a massive, ghastly tornado of energy and debris. Close-up on the rocket, dramatic low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist, now wearing a flowing cloak, stands triumphantly on a recovered chronometer, observing a disabled, virus-ridden trawler in the distance, against a backdrop of glowing Eastern-style futuristic cityscapes and peaceful nightlife. Full shot, heroic pose, high angle.

definitions.definition: An area of land that is at elevation; mountainous land.
Relating to highlands.
Heavy, strong, vigorous, mighty, impressive.

definitions.definition: Strong; bulky.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Possessing physical strength and weight; rugged and powerful; powerfully or heavily built.
definitions.example: He was a tall, hefty man.

definitions.definition: Heavy, weighing a lot.
definitions.example: She carries a hefty backpack full of books.

definitions.definition: (of a number or amount) Large.
definitions.example: That's going to cost you a hefty sum.

definitions.definition: A prepubescent child.
A person younger than the age of majority; a minor.
A person younger than the age of full criminal responsibility, such that the person either cannot be held criminally liable or is subject to less severe forms of punishment.
A publication for young adult readers.
An actor playing a child's role.
A sexually immature animal.
A two-year-old racehorse.
A restaurant in which customers select their food at a counter then carry it on a tray to a table to eat
A dining area in an institution where meals may be purchased (as above), provided, or brought in from elsewhere
Taking care; attentive to potential danger, error or harm; cautious.

definitions.definition: Conscientious and painstaking; meticulous.
definitions.example: They made a careful search of the crime scene.

definitions.definition: Full of care or grief; sorrowful, sad.
Full of cares or anxiety; worried, troubled.
A group of people or vehicles, generally armed, who go with a person or people of importance to safeguard them on a journey or mission.
An accompanying person in such a group.
A guard who travels with a dangerous person, such as a criminal, for the protection of others.
A group of people attending as a mark of respect or honor.
An accompanying person in a social gathering, etc.
Protection, care, or safeguard on a journey or excursion.
(somewhat euphemistic) A sex worker who does not operate in a brothel, but with whom clients make appointments; a call girl or male equivalent.
To attend to in order to guard and protect; to accompany as a safeguard (for the person escorted or for others); to give honorable or ceremonious attendance to
To accompany (a person) in order to compel them to go somewhere (e.g. to leave a building).

definitions.definition: To go with someone as a partner, for example on a formal date.
A piece of jewelry worn on the ear.
A public gathering or mass meeting that is not mainly a protest and is organized to inspire enthusiasm for a cause.

definitions.definition: A protest or demonstration for or against something, but often with speeches and often without marching, especially in North America.
definitions.example: a political rally

definitions.definition: A sequence of strokes between serving and scoring a point.
An event in which competitors drive through a series of timed special stages at intervals. The winner is the driver who completes all stages with the shortest cumulative time.
A recovery after a decline in prices (said of the market, stocks, etc.)
To collect, and reduce to order, as troops dispersed or thrown into confusion; to gather again; to reunite.
To come into orderly arrangement; to renew order, or united effort, as troops scattered or put to flight; to assemble; to unite.
To collect one's vital powers or forces; to regain health or consciousness; to recuperate.
To recover strength after a decline in prices; -- said of the market, stocks, etc.
To tease; to chaff good-humouredly.
An American black bear, Ursus americanus, indigenous to North America.
An Asiatic black bear, Ursus thibetanus.
To travel through thick wooded country, cutting away scrub to make progress
To fight, as a guerilla, especially in wooded country
To ambush
To create a visual representation of a territory, etc. via cartography.
(followed by a "to" phrase) To act as a function on something, taking it to something else.
(followed by a "to" phrase) To have a direct relationship; to correspond.
To inform someone of a particular idea.
A device, generally constructed from fabric, that is designed to employ air resistance to control the fall of an object.
A web or fold of skin extending between the legs of gliding mammals, such as the flying squirrel and colugo.
(BDSM) A small collar which fastens around the scrotum and from which weights can be hung.
To jump, fall, descend, etc. using such a device.
To introduce into a place using such a device.
definitions.example: The soldiers were parachuted behind enemy lines.

definitions.definition: To place (somebody) in an organisation in a position of authority without their having previous experience there; used with in or into.
An alcoholic liquor distilled from fermented grain and usually aged in oak barrels.
A drink of this liquor.
A light gig or carriage.
Any apparatus used to maintain environmental conditions suitable for a reaction.

An apparatus used to maintain environmental conditions suitable for the hatching of eggs.
A place to maintain the culturing of bacteria at a steady temperature.
A support programme for the development of entrepreneurial companies.
A woman with an excessive libido.

definitions.definition: (of a woman) Having an excessive libido.
Somebody who possesses great bravery and carries out extraordinary or noble deeds.
A role model.
The protagonist in a work of fiction.
The current player, especially an hypothetical player for example and didactic purposes. Compare: villain. Not to be confused with hero call.

definitions.definition: A large sandwich made from meats and cheeses; a hero sandwich.
(food styling) The product chosen from several candidates to be photographed.
The eye-catching top portion of a web page, sometimes including a hero image; the portion above the fold.
To completely remove, get rid of, put an end to.
To kill (a person or animal).

definitions.definition: To excrete (waste products).

A thing that has physical existence.
Objective; the goal, end or purpose of something.
(grammar) The noun phrase which is an internal complement of a verb phrase or a prepositional phrase. In a verb phrase with a transitive action verb, it is typically the receiver of the action.
A person or thing toward which an emotion is directed.
definitions.example: Mary Jane had been the object of Peter's affection for years.

definitions.definition: An instantiation of a class or structure.
An element within a category upon which functions operate. Thus, a category consists of a set of element objects and the functions that operate on them.
Sight; show; appearance; aspect.
A means of exit, escape, reprieve, etc.

definitions.definition: A state in which a member of the batting team is removed from play due to the application of various rules of the game such as striking out, hitting a fly ball which is caught by the fielding team before bouncing, etc.

A dismissal; a state in which a member of the batting team finishes his turn at bat, due to the application of various rules of the game, such as the bowler knocking over the batsman's wicket with the ball.
A card which can make a hand a winner.
A trip out; an outing.
(chiefly in the plural) One who, or that which, is out; especially, one who is out of office.

To reveal (a person) as LGBT+ (gay, trans, etc).
To reveal (a person or organization) as having a certain secret, such as a being a secret agent or undercover detective.

definitions.definition: To come or go out; to get out or away; to become public or apparent.

definitions.definition: Not inside or within something.

definitions.definition: Freed from confinement or secrecy.

definitions.definition: Not fitted or inserted into something.

definitions.definition: Of the ball or other playing implement, falling or passing outside the bounds of the playing area.

definitions.definition: Released, available for purchase, download or other use.

definitions.definition: (in various games; used especially of a batsman or batter in cricket or baseball) Dismissed from play under the rules of the game.

definitions.definition: Openly acknowledging that one is LGBT+ (gay, trans, etc).

definitions.definition: (by extension) Open, public; public about or openly acknowledging some (usually specified) identity.
(of flowers) In bloom.

definitions.definition: (of the sun, moon or stars) Visible in the sky; not obscured by clouds.

definitions.definition: (of lamps, fires etc.) Not shining or burning.
definitions.example: I called round to the house but all the lights were out and no one was home.

definitions.definition: (of ideas, plans, etc.) Discarded; no longer a possibility.

definitions.definition: (of certain services, devices, or facilities) Not available; out of service.

definitions.definition: (of an organization, etc.) Temporarily not in operation, or not being attended as usual.

definitions.definition: Of the tide, at or near its lowest level.

definitions.definition: No longer popular or in fashion.

definitions.definition: Without; no longer in possession of; not having more

definitions.definition: (of calculations or measurements) Containing errors or discrepancies; in error by a stated amount.

definitions.definition: Of a young lady: having entered society and available to be courted.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Disturbing or unnerving, often in such a way that others may suspect them of intending physical or sexual harm or harassment.

Damage to the body of a human or animal.

definitions.definition: 10-20 (location).
definitions.example: What’s your twenty, good buddy?

definitions.definition: An old English division of infantry.
The cardinal number 20, occurring after nineteen and before twenty-one.
A device that directs and controls energy, often in the form of movement or electricity, to produce a certain effect.
A vehicle operated mechanically, such as an automobile or an airplane.
(abbreviation) An answering machine or, by extension, voice mail.
definitions.example: I called you earlier, but all I got was the machine.

definitions.definition: A computer.
definitions.example: Game developers assume they're pushing the limits of the machine.

definitions.definition: A person or organisation that seemingly acts like a machine, being particularly efficient, single-minded, or unemotional.
definitions.example: Bruce Campbell was a "demon-killing machine" because he made quick work of killing demons.

definitions.definition: Especially, the group that controls a political or similar organization; a combination of persons acting together for a common purpose, with the agencies which they use.
Supernatural agency in a poem, or a superhuman being introduced to perform some exploit.
The system of special interest groups that supports a political party, especially in urban areas.
Penis.
A contrivance in the Ancient Greek theatre for indicating a change of scene, by means of which a god might cross the stage or deliver a divine message; the deus ex machina.
A bathing machine.
To make by machinery.
To shape or finish by machinery.

A disorder of the immune system causing adverse reactions to substances (allergens) not harmful to most and marked by the body's production of histamines and associated with atopy, anaphylaxis, and asthma.
Any condition of hypersensitivity to a substance.
Altered susceptibility to a first treatment as exhibited in reaction to a subsequent one.
An antipathy, as toward a person or activity.
Whatever.
In any way; at all.
definitions.example: He gave me no answer whatsoever.

definitions.definition: Whatever
In the context of which the present document or ruling is made.
definitions.example: the within appeal

definitions.definition: In or into the interior; inside.
In the inner part, spatially; physically inside.
definitions.example: within her studio

definitions.definition: In the scope or range of.
definitions.example: within his hearing;  her  within five seconds of breaking the record;  within an inch of falling overboard

definitions.definition: Before the specified duration ends.
definitions.example: Leave here within three days.

definitions.definition: Any member of a race of beings from (especially Scandinavian and other Germanic) folklore, usually depicted as having some sort of supernatural powers and being skilled in crafting and metalworking, often as short with long beards, and sometimes as clashing with elves.
A person of short stature, often one whose limbs are disproportionately small in relation to the body as compared with normal adults, usually as the result of a genetic condition.
An animal, plant or other thing much smaller than the usual of its sort.
definitions.example: dwarf honeysuckle

definitions.definition: A star of relatively small size.
To render (much) smaller, turn into a dwarf (version).
To make appear (much) smaller, puny, tiny.
definitions.example: The newly-built skyscraper dwarfs all older buildings in the downtown skyline.

definitions.definition: To make appear insignificant.
definitions.example: Bach dwarfs all other composers.

definitions.definition: To become (much) smaller.
To hinder from growing to the natural size; to make or keep small; to stunt.
Miniature.
definitions.example: It is possible to grow the plants as dwarf as one desires.

definitions.definition: To conceive or carry out a secret plan intended to harm; to form a plot or scheme.
To arouse the interest of; to fascinate.
To have clandestine or illicit intercourse.
To fill with artifice and duplicity; to complicate.
Freedom from some business or activity.
Free time given over to a specific purpose; occupation, activity.
A period during which official activity or business is formally suspended; an official holiday from university, law courts etc.
A holiday; a stretch of leisure time away from work or duty and devoted to rest or pleasure.
The act of vacating something; moving out.
definitions.example: The Conservative Party’s vacation of the centre ground gave an opportunity to its opponents.

definitions.definition: The act of making legally void.
To spend or take a vacation.
definitions.example: This year, we’re vacationing in Mexico.

definitions.definition: One who follows, comes after another.
Something that comes after another thing.
One who is a part of master's physical group, such as a servant or retainer.
One who follows mentally, adherer to the opinions, ideas or teachings of another, a movement etc.
An imitator, who follows another's example.
A pursuer.
An account holder who subscribes to see content from another account on a social media platform.
definitions.example: I have over 50 followers on Twitter, but all my tweets only get 10 likes.

definitions.definition: A machine part receiving motion from another.
A man courting a maidservant.
Young cattle.
A metal piece placed at the top of a candle to keep the wax melting evenly.
Any of the three players (the ruckman, ruck rover, and rover) who usually follow the ball around the ground rather than occupying a fixed position.
A debt collector.
A pacifier.
Being something which sucks, which is unpleasant or inferior.
definitions.example: That comedian was pretty sucky on TV last night.

definitions.definition: A moon or other smaller body orbiting a larger one.
definitions.example: A spent upper stage is a derelict satellite.

definitions.definition: A man-made apparatus designed to be placed in orbit around a celestial body, generally to relay information, data etc. to Earth.
definitions.example: Many telecommunication satellites orbit at 36000km above the equator.

definitions.definition: A country, state, office, building etc. which is under the jurisdiction, influence, or domination of another body.
An attendant on an important person; a member of someone's retinue, often in a somewhat derogatory sense; a henchman.
Satellite TV; reception of television broadcasts via services that utilize man-made satellite technology.
definitions.example: Do you have satellite at your house?

definitions.definition: (grammar) A grammatical construct that takes various forms and may encode a path of movement, a change of state, or the grammatical aspect. Examples: "a bird flew past"; "she turned on the light".
To transmit by satellite.
An instance of hijacking; the illegal seizure of a vehicle; a hijacking.
An instance of a seizure and redirection of a process.
An amendment which deletes the contents of a bill and inserts entirely new provisions.
Preflop, the position two before the dealer.
To forcibly stop and seize control of some vehicle in order to rob it or to reach a destination (especially an airplane, truck or a boat).
To seize control of some process or resource to achieve a purpose other than its originally intended one.
To seize control of a networked computer by means of infecting it with a worm or other malware, thereby turning it into a zombie.
To change software settings without a user's knowledge so as to force that user to visit a certain web site (to hijack a browser).
To introduce an amendment deleting the contents of a bill and inserting entirely new provisions.
The civilian sphere as opposed to the military; or specifically the civilian sphere which supports a military effort
A sweet mixture of fruit boiled with sugar and allowed to congeal. Often spread on bread or toast or used in jam tarts.
A difficult situation.
Blockage, congestion.
definitions.example: A traffic jam caused us to miss the game's first period.

definitions.definition: An informal, impromptu performance or rehearsal.
(by extension) A song; a track.
(by extension) An informal event where people brainstorm and collaborate on projects.
To get something stuck in a confined space.
definitions.example: Her poor little baby toe got jammed in the door.

definitions.definition: To brusquely force something into a space; cram, squeeze.
definitions.example: The rush-hour train was jammed with commuters.

definitions.definition: To cause congestion or blockage. Often used with "up"
definitions.example: A single accident can jam the roads for hours.

definitions.definition: To block or confuse a broadcast signal.
To throw a pitch at or near the batter's hands.
definitions.example: Jones was jammed by the pitch.

definitions.definition: To play music (especially improvisation as a group, or an informal unrehearsed session).
To injure a finger or toe by sudden compression of the digit's tip.
definitions.example: When he tripped on the step he jammed his toe.

definitions.definition: To attempt to score points.
definitions.example: Toughie jammed four times in the second period.

definitions.definition: To bring (a vessel) so close to the wind that half her upper sails are laid aback.
To give up on a date or some joint endeavour; stand up, chicken out, jam out.
A large flightless bird (Struthio camelus) native to Africa.
One who buries one's head in the sand instead of acknowledging problems
(usually followed by to or of) Lacking awareness; unmindful; unaware, unconscious of.
Failing to remember; forgetful.

A solemn, authentic instrument in writing, by which a person declares his or her will as to disposal of his or her inheritance (estate and effects) after his or her death, benefiting specified heir(s).

One of the two parts to the scriptures of the Christian religion: the New Testament, considered by Christians to be a continuation of the Hebrew scriptures, and the Hebrew scriptures themselves, which they refer to as the Old Testament.
A tangible proof or tribute.
definitions.example: The ancient aqueducts are a testament to the great engineering skill of the Roman Empire.

definitions.definition: A credo, expression of conviction
definitions.example: The prime minister's speech was a glowing testament to the cabinet's undying commitment to the royal cause.

definitions.definition: Resembling milk in color, consistency, smell, etc.; consisting of milk.
(color science) Of the black in an image, appearing as dark gray rather than black.
(of a drink) Containing (an especially large amount of) milk.
definitions.example: milky tea; milky cocoa

definitions.definition: (of grains) Containing a whitish liquid, juicy.
Cowardly.
Immature, childish.
Producing milk, lactating.
Hatred of, contempt for, or prejudice against women.
One of a set of parallel lines.
Direction conformable to that of another line.
A line of latitude.
definitions.example: The 31st parallel passes through the center of my town.

definitions.definition: An arrangement of electrical components such that a current flows along two or more paths; see in parallel.
Something identical or similar in essential respects.
A comparison made; elaborate tracing of similarity.
definitions.example: Johnson's parallel between Dryden and Pope

definitions.definition: One of a series of long trenches constructed before a besieged fortress, by the besieging force, as a cover for troops supporting the attacking batteries. They are roughly parallel to the line of outer defenses of the fortress.
A character consisting of two parallel vertical lines, used in the text to direct attention to a similarly marked note in the margin or at the foot of a page.
To construct or place something parallel to something else.
Of a path etc: To be parallel to something else.
Of a process etc: To be analogous to something else.
To compare or liken something to something else.
To make to conform to something else in character, motive, aim, etc.
To equal; to match; to correspond to.
To produce or adduce as a parallel.
Equally distant from one another at all points.
definitions.example: The horizontal lines on my notebook paper are parallel.

definitions.definition: Having the same overall direction; the comparison is indicated with "to".
definitions.example: The two railway lines are parallel.

definitions.definition: (hyperbolic geometry, said of a pair of lines) Either not intersecting, or coinciding.
Involving the processing of multiple tasks at the same time.
definitions.example: a parallel algorithm

definitions.definition: With a parallel relationship.
definitions.example: The road runs parallel to the canal.

definitions.definition: An evil supernatural spirit.
A neutral supernatural spirit.
Someone with great strength, passion or skill for a particular activity, pursuit etc.; an enthusiast.
definitions.example: He’s a demon at the card tables.

definitions.definition: A form of patience (known as Canfield in the US).
Any of various hesperiid butterflies of the genera Notocrypta and Udaspes.
A fool, a simpleton, a mindless person.
A small mindless sketch, etc.
(sometimes childish) Penis.
To draw or scribble aimlessly.
definitions.example: The bored student doodled a submarine in his notebook.

definitions.definition: To drone like a bagpipe.
An institution which holds books and/or other forms of media for use by the public or qualified people often lending them out, as well as providing various other services for its users.
(by extension) Any institution that lends out its goods for use by the public or a community.
A collection of books or other forms of stored information.
An equivalent collection of analogous information in a non-printed form, e.g. record library.
A room dedicated to storing books.
A collection of software routines that provide functionality to be incorporated into or used by a computer program.
A collection of DNA material from a single organism or relative to a single disease.
The deck or draw pile.
A portion of land or territory which the eye can comprehend in a single view, including all the objects it contains.
A sociological aspect of a physical area.
A picture representing a real or imaginary scene by land or sea, the main subject being the general aspect of nature, as fields, hills, forests, water, etc.
The pictorial aspect of a country.
A mode of printing where the horizontal sides are longer than the vertical sides
A space, indoor or outdoor and natural or man-made (as in "designed landscape")
A situation that is presented, a scenario
definitions.example: The software patent landscape has changed considerably in the last years

definitions.definition: To create or maintain a landscape.
That which comes from one's imagination.
The literary genre generally dealing with themes of magic and the supernatural, imaginary worlds and creatures, etc.
A fantastical design.
The drug gamma-hydroxybutyric acid.
Nocturnal activities, especially visiting nightclubs.
definitions.example: Rio de Janeiro boasts the best nightlife in South America.

definitions.definition: (Greek, Roman) Any female nature spirit associated with water, forests, grotto, wind, etc.
A young girl, especially one who is attractive, beautiful or graceful.
The larva of certain insects.
Any of various butterflies of the family Nymphalidae.
An effigy, typically made of straw and dressed in old clothes, fixed to a pole in a field to deter birds from eating seeds or crops planted there.
A tall, thin, awkward person.
Anything that appears terrifying but presents no danger.
A person clad in rags and tatters.
A bird, the black tern.
To splay rigidly outward, like the arms of a scarecrow.
A device for measuring time, such as a watch or clock.
That which has a distinct existence as an individual unit. Often used for organisations which have no physical form.
The existence of something considered apart from its properties.
Anything about which information or data can be stored in a database; in particular, an organised array or set of individual elements or parts.
The state or quality of being or existence.
definitions.example: The group successfully maintains its tribal entity.

definitions.definition: A spirit, ghost, or the like.
An alien lifeform that has no corporeal body.
An other, another (person, etc), more often rendered as another.
definitions.example: I'm afraid little Robbie does not always play well with others.

definitions.definition: The other one; the second of two.
definitions.example: One boat is not better than the other.

definitions.definition: To regard, label or treat as an "other", as not part of the same group; to view as different and alien.
To treat as different or separate; segregate; ostracise.
See other (determiner) below
Second.
definitions.example: I get paid every other week.

definitions.definition: Alien.
Different.
Left, as opposed to right.
Apart from; in the phrase "other than".
definitions.example: Other than that, I'm fine.

definitions.definition: Otherwise.
definitions.example: If you think other. — Shakespeare.

definitions.definition: An offside play.
The side of a road vehicle furthest from the kerb: the right side if one drives on the left of the road.
definitions.example: My offside wing mirror got snapped off.

definitions.definition: The right-hand side of a working animal such as a horse or bullock, especially when in harness.
(of a canal) The side opposite the towpath.
In an illegal position ahead of the ball, puck, etc.
To the side of the road, past the curb and sidewalk, e.g. an offside diner (restaurant.)
Unfavourably located, from the point of view of the player taking a finesse.
To the side of a boat, opposite the primary side on which one paddles.
A violent windstorm characterized by a mobile, twisting, funnel-shaped cloud.
definitions.example: A tornado is a rotating column of air.

definitions.definition: A catapult or other kind of war-engine.
Torture, originally as inflicted by an instrument of torture.
Any extreme pain, anguish or misery, either physical or mental.
definitions.example: He was bitter from the torments of the divorce system.

definitions.definition: To cause severe suffering to (stronger than to vex but weaker than to torture.)
definitions.example: The child tormented the flies by pulling their wings off.

definitions.definition: Having or showing pluck, courage or spirit in trying circumstances.
One who practices alchemy.
One who blends material or substances in the nature or supposed nature of alchemy.
To fail to agree; to have a different opinion or belief.
definitions.example: Bob says cats are friendlier than dogs, but I disagree.

definitions.definition: To fail to conform or correspond with.
definitions.example: My results in the laboratory consistently disagree with yours.

definitions.definition: To consume (a liquid) through the mouth.
definitions.example: He drank the water I gave him.

definitions.definition: (metonymic) To consume the liquid contained within (a bottle, glass, etc.).
definitions.example: Jack drank the whole bottle by himself.

definitions.definition: To consume alcoholic beverages.
definitions.example: Everyone who is drinking is drinking, but not everyone who is drinking is drinking.

definitions.definition: To take in (a liquid), in any manner; to suck up; to absorb; to imbibe.
To take in; to receive within one, through the senses; to inhale; to hear; to see.
To smoke, as tobacco.
One who is intoxicated with alcohol.
A habitual drinker, especially one who is frequently intoxicated.
A drinking-bout; a period of drunkenness.
A drunken state.
Intoxicated as a result of excessive alcohol consumption, usually by drinking alcoholic beverages.
Habitually or frequently in a state of intoxication.
(usually followed by with or on) Elated or emboldened.
definitions.example: Drunk with power he immediately ordered a management reshuffle.

definitions.definition: Drenched or saturated with moisture or liquid.
A legendary serpentine or reptilian creature.
An animal of various species that resemble a dragon in appearance:
(with definite article, often capitalized) The constellation Draco.
A fierce and unpleasant woman; a harridan.
definitions.example: She’s a bit of a dragon.

definitions.definition: (with definite article, often capitalized) The (historical) Chinese empire or the People's Republic of China.
definitions.example: Napoleon already warned of the awakening of the Dragon.

definitions.definition: Something very formidable or dangerous.
A type of playing-tile (red dragon, green dragon, white dragon) in the game of mahjong.
A luminous exhalation from marshy ground, seeming to move through the air like a winged serpent.
A short musket hooked to a swivel attached to a soldier's belt; so called from a representation of a dragon's head at the muzzle.
A background process similar to a daemon.
A variety of carrier pigeon.
A transvestite man, or more broadly a male-to-female transgender person.
Damage to the body of a human or animal.
definitions.example: The passenger sustained a severe injury in the car accident.

definitions.definition: The violation of a person's reputation, rights, property, or interests.
definitions.example: Slander is an injury to the character.

definitions.definition: Injustice.
The act of injecting, or something that is injected.
A specimen prepared by injection.
A morphism from either one of the two components of a coproduct to that coproduct.
The act of inserting materials like concrete grout or gravel by using high pressure pumps.
The supply of additional funding to a person or a business.
definitions.example: The troubled business received a much-needed cash injection.

definitions.definition: A relation on sets (X,Y) that associates each element of Y with at most one element of X.
The insertion of program code into an application, URL, hardware, etc.; especially when malicious or when the target is not designed for such insertion.
definitions.example: a SQL injection exploit allowing a malicious user to modify a database query

definitions.definition: The act of putting a spacecraft into a particular orbit, especially for changing a stable orbit into a transfer orbit, e.g. trans-lunar injection.
A function that maps distinct x in the domain to distinct y in the codomain; formally, a f: X → Y such that f(a) = f(b) implies a = b for any a, b in the domain.
(specifically) Something injected subcutaneously, intravenously, or intramuscularly by use of a syringe and a needle.
(steam engines) The act of throwing cold water into a condenser to produce a vacuum.
(steam engines) The cold water thrown into a condenser to produce a vacuum.
The grantee of a fief, feud, or fee; one who keeps land of a superior, and who vows fidelity and homage to him, normally a lord of a manor; a feudatory; a feudal tenant.
A subordinate
To treat as a vassal or to reduce to the position of a vassal; to subject to control; to enslave.
To subordinate to someone or something.
Resembling a vassal; slavish; servile.
A submicroscopic, non-cellular structure consisting of a core of DNA or RNA surrounded by a protein coat, that requires a living host cell to replicate, and often causes disease in the host organism.
A disease caused by these organisms.
definitions.example: He caught a virus and had to stay home from school.

definitions.definition: Venom, as produced by a poisonous animal etc.

A type of malware which can covertly transmit itself between computers via networks (especially the Internet) or removable storage such as disks, often causing damage to systems and data; also computer virus.
Any type of malware.
The quality of smoothness of flow.
The quality of being fluent in a language; A person's command of a particular language.
The quality of consistently applying skill correctly in the manner of one well-practiced at it, requiring little deliberate thought to perform without mistakes
One who kicks.
One who takes kicks.
The kicking strap.
An outboard motor.
An unexpected situation, detail or circumstance, often unpleasant.
definitions.example: John wants to climb the wall, but the kicker is that it is thirty feet tall.

definitions.definition: An enticement for investors, e.g. warranty added to the investment contract.
An unpaired card which is part of a pair, two pair, or three of a kind poker hand.
definitions.example: Jill's hand was two pair, aces and sevens, with a king kicker.

definitions.definition: Small text above a headline that indicates the topic of the story.
The last one or two paragraphs of a story.
A lighthearted or humorous item used to round off a news broadcast.
A device that periodically displaces a newspaper from the print production line, to aid in gathering the newspapers into fixed-size bundles.
A launch ramp.
The fermenting mass of fruit that is the basis of pruno, or "prison wine".
A relaxed party.
A backlight positioned at an angle.
An introduction; something that leads into the beginning of something.
definitions.example: After the long lead-in, the climax of the story was a disappointment.

definitions.definition: A region of data at the beginning of a compact disc, holding the table of contents.
A short phrase that begins the caption of a photograph.
To make melancholy; to sadden or grieve (someone).
(heading) Emotionally negative.
Sated, having had one's fill; satisfied, weary.
Steadfast, valiant.
Dignified, serious, grave.
Naughty; troublesome; wicked.
Unfashionable; socially inadequate or undesirable.
definitions.example: I can't believe you use drugs; you're so sad!

definitions.definition: Soggy (to refer to pastries).
Heavy; weighty; ponderous; close; hard.
definitions.example: sad bread

definitions.definition: Glass that has been coloured, either by painting or by fusing pigments into its structure.
The use of such glass to construct decorative windows, especially in churches.
A long outer garment worn over the shoulders covering the back; a cape, often with a hood.
A blanket-like covering, often metaphorical.
definitions.example: Night hid her movements with its cloak of darkness.

definitions.definition: That which conceals; a disguise or pretext.
A text replacement for an IRC user's hostname or IP address, making the user less identifiable.
To cover as with a cloak.
To hide or conceal.
To render or become invisible via futuristic technology.
definitions.example: The ship cloaked before entering the enemy sector of space.

definitions.definition: The legal right to take care of something or somebody, especially children.
definitions.example: The court awarded custody to the child's father.

definitions.definition: Temporary possession or care of somebody else's property.
definitions.example: I couldn't pay the bill and now my passport is in custody of the hotel management.

definitions.definition: The state of being imprisoned or detained, usually pending a trial.
definitions.example: He was mistreated while in police custody.

definitions.definition: An area under the jurisdiction of a custos within the Order of Friars Minor.
definitions.example: The Custody of the Holy Land includes the monasteries of Bethlehem, Nazareth, and Jerusalem.

definitions.definition: An area of the earth's surface between two parallels of latitude.
A region of the Earth.

The long-term manifestations of weather and other atmospheric conditions in a given area or country, now usually represented by the statistical summary of its weather conditions during a period long enough to ensure that representative values are obtained (generally 30 years).
The context in general of a particular political, moral etc. situation.
definitions.example: Industries that require a lot of fossil fuels are unlikely to be popular in the current political climate.

definitions.definition: To dwell.
Broody.
Prone to cluck.
A shift (scheduled work period) that takes place early in the day.
At a time in advance of the usual or expected event.
definitions.example: at eleven, we went for an early lunch;  she began reading at an early age;  his mother suffered an early death

definitions.definition: Arriving a time before expected; sooner than on time.
definitions.example: The early guests sipped their punch and avoided each other's eyes.

definitions.definition: Near the start or beginning.
definitions.example: Early results showed their winning 245 out of 300 seats in parliament. The main opponent locked up only 31 seats.

definitions.definition: Having begun to occur; in its early stages.
definitions.example: early cancer

definitions.definition: Of a star or class of stars, hotter than the sun.
At a time before expected; sooner than usual.
definitions.example: We finished the project an hour sooner than scheduled, so we left early.

definitions.definition: Soon; in good time; seasonably.
A shaking of the ground, caused by volcanic activity or movement around geologic faults.
(planetary geology) Such a quake specifically occurring on the planet Earth, as opposed to other celestial bodies.
A rocket engine.
A non-guided missile propelled by a rocket engine.
A vehicle propelled by a rocket engine.
A rocket propelled firework, a skyrocket
An ace (the playing card).
An angry communication (such as a letter or telegram) to a subordinate.
A blunt lance head used in jousting.
Something that shoots high in the air.
A stupid or crazy person.
To accelerate swiftly and powerfully
To fly vertically
To rise or soar rapidly
To carry something in a rocket
To attack something with rockets
A fishing boat that uses a trawl net or dragnet to catch fish.
A fisherman who uses a trawl net.
